2605.04922.title: Evolving Idea Graphs with Learnable Edits-and-Commits for Multi-Agent Scientific Ideation
2605.04922.paper_summary: LLM-empowered multi-agent systems offer new potential to accelerate scientific discovery by generating novel research ideas. However, existing methods typically coordinate agents through temporary texts, such as drafts or chat logs; it is difficult to pinpoint the weaknesses in the generated ideas and how the agents refine them. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Evolving Idea Graphs} (EIG), a graph-based multi-agent scientific ideation framework that can generate high-performance research ideas across various benchmark-native metrics, such as novelty, feasibility, and clarity. Instead of coordinating solely through texts, EIG represents a partially formed proposal as an evolving idea graph, where nodes capture scientific claims and edges encode relations (e.g., support and conflict), enabling unresolved weaknesses to remain identifiable throughout the idea evolving process. Specifically, a learned two-head controller operates over the evolving graph to guide the ideation: one head selects graph edits for agents to execute, while the other decides when the graph is ready for commit as final proposal synthesis. On AI Idea Bench 2025 and LiveIdeaBench, EIG outperforms all compared systems on both automatic benchmark scores and blind expert ratings. Ablations further show that explicit graph state provides the main performance gains, and learned edit-and-commit control adds consistent improvements.
2605.04922.paper_summary_zh: LLM 驅動的多代理系統為加速科學發現提供了新的潛力，通過生成新穎的研究想法。<lb/>然而，現有的方法通常通過臨時文本（如草稿或聊天記錄）來協調代理；很難確定生成想法的弱點以及代理如何改進它們。<lb/>為此，我們引入了 \textbf{Evolving Idea Graphs} (EIG)，這是一個基於圖形的多代理科學構思框架，能夠在各種基準原生指標（如新穎性、可行性和清晰度）上生成高性能的研究想法。<lb/>EIG 不僅僅通過文本進行協調，而是將部分形成的提案表示為一個不斷演變的想法圖，其中節點捕捉科學主張，邊緣編碼關係（例如支持和衝突），使未解決的弱點在想法演變過程中保持可識別。<lb/>具體而言，一個學習的雙頭控制器在不斷演變的圖上運作以指導構思：一個頭選擇代理執行的圖編輯，而另一個則決定何時圖準備好提交作為最終提案的綜合。<lb/>在 AI Idea Bench 2025 和 LiveIdeaBench 上，EIG 在自動基準分數和盲專家評分方面均優於所有比較系統。<lb/>消融實驗進一步顯示，明確的圖狀態提供了主要的性能增益，而學習的編輯和提交控制則帶來了一致的改進。
2605.04922.author: Jiangwen Dong et.al.
2605.04922.authors: Jiangwen Dong, Bo Li, Wanyu Lin
